project_overview.project_name: Getting Results: Access and Delivery of Quality Education Services in Balochistan (GRADES-Balochistan)
project_overview.project_development_objective: Increase girls' and boys' enrollment in pre-primary and primary grades, and improve reading and numeracy proficiency in primary grades, all in project-supported schools
project_overview.beneficiaries: 240,000 students (90,000 in ECE, 150,000 in primary); 5,000 teachers through CPD; 400 female scholarship recipients; parents and communities in Balochistan

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Balochistan is affected by security challenges and conflict-related risks; project includes security management plan and remote supervision mechanisms

key_components.description: Education reform interventions including: ECE quality enhancement (US$4.92M), double-shift introduction (US$2M), PPP school establishment (US$4.73M), community schools (US$3.27M), CPD for teachers (US$6.8M), teacher recruitment and career management policy (US$8M)

Infrastructure development and access barriers removal: climate-smart classroom construction/rehabilitation (US$59.88M), parental engagement programs (US$1.4M), safe and affordable school transportation for girls (US$2M)

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Technical Assistance, Evaluation and Project Implementation
key_components.description: Project management, M&E, capacity building, functional review of SED, third-party verification, impact evaluations
Provision for immediate response to eligible crisis or emergency as needed

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: School Education Department (SED), Government of Balochistan; Planning and Development Department as borrower contact
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) chaired by Additional Chief Secretary (Development), meeting biannually; Project Coordination Committee (PCC) meeting quarterly; coordination with PITE, B-PPPA, Transportation Department, PPIU, and development partners (UNICEF, UNESCO)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers; some activities may follow provincial procurement procedures through national competition; STEP system for tracking; PPSD developed and approved; open competition required with public notices; pre-bid conferences for bidder familiarization
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PMU responsible for overall M&E; biannual reporting to World Bank and PSC; Third-Party Verification (TPV) for PBC achievement; EMIS for enrollment data; National Achievement Test (NAT) for learning outcomes at baseline (2026), midline (2028), and endline (2030); community scorecards in selected districts; GEMS for remote supervision; impact evaluations for parental engagement and transportation interventions

identified_risks.risk_description: Frequent transfers of government officials working on the project may slow implementation despite project remaining a priority
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: PSC will facilitate inter-agency cooperation and help manage political and governance changes as they arise

identified_risks.risk_description: Pakistan faces substantial macroeconomic risks including slow growth, limited policy buffers, potential worsening of external conditions, further natural disasters, and slowdown in policy reforms
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government commitment to IMF Extended Fund Facility program implementation; World Bank support to structural policy reforms in fiscal and power sectors

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: SED's first experience with Performance-Based Conditions may lead to implementation challenges; risk of government failing to hire enough teachers or increasing burden on current teachers through double shifts; no government contribution to project implementation creates sustainability risk
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Thorough consultations on technical design and implementation; strong capacity-building and implementation support; early teacher hiring initiation; revised Teacher Hiring and Career Management Policy; PBCs supporting private sector participation and scholarships for female teachers; sustainability targets linked to government budget inclusion post-project closure

identified_risks.risk_category: Technical Design of Project or Program
identified_risks.risk_description: Substantial teacher shortage in Balochistan; project focuses on quality improvement but relies on government for teacher recruitment and salaries; risk of project investments being discontinued after closure without government contribution
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Early teacher hiring initiation; revised Teacher Hiring and Career Management Policy to increase profession attractiveness; PBCs for private sector participation and female teacher scholarships; sustainability measures requiring activities to be reflected in government budget following project closure

identified_risks.risk_description: Key policies such as double-shift policy and teacher recruitment and career management policy may face resistance
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Specific policy reforms supported via PBCs; alliance with key stakeholders and champions in SED and Ministry of Education to advocate for required policy shifts

identified_risks.risk_description: Construction and rehabilitation activities may result in temporary environmental impacts and OHS risks; social risks include potential exclusion of marginalized groups, gender disparities, increased likelihood of GBV and VAC, land acquisition impacts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF prepared with systematic screening process; resettlement procedure for land impacts; OHS protocols; security management; GRM established; stakeholder engagement plan; inclusive education principles integrated; capacity building for SED on ESF requirements; targeted training on GBV/SEA/SH risk management

identified_risks.risk_description: Allocation of inadequate funds and delays in fund release by Finance Department; no functional internal audit in SED
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: SED committed to timely processing of payments; IUFR-based disbursements; separate fund center for project expenditure tracking; internal audit firm to be hired with semi-annual audits in first year; Auditor General of Pakistan to conduct external audits

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential resistance from communities to project activities; social norms and cultural practices may prevent girls' enrollment
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Stakeholder Engagement Plan; community meetings in accessible locations; focused group discussions with gender and disability inclusivity; materials in local languages and accessible formats; community scorecards for monitoring; GRM for grievance redress

identified_risks.risk_description: Current security situation in Balochistan may restrict or prevent access to project areas, affecting implementation and supervision
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security management plan to be developed; security measures included in work contracts for sensitive areas; strong third-party monitoring; remote supervision using GEMS; remote in-service training via technology

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM established; sector-wide GRM under previous World Bank projects; community scorecards in selected districts; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service) available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project targets at least 50% female enrollment in ECE and primary; scholarships for 400 female high-school graduates to pursue teaching degrees and return to underserved areas; teacher recruitment and career management policy includes quotas for female recruitment and rural hardship allowances; safe and affordable school transportation for girls with 75% beneficiary target being girls; female representation in PTSMCs; girls' schools prioritized for infrastructure upgrades; double-shift policy promotes opening girls' shifts in boys' schools; gender-balanced and sensitized PTSMCs to contract transport providers
vulnerable_groups: Out-of-school children (2.9 million in Balochistan, 58% of child population)
Children in remote and geographically challenging areas

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project design incorporates conflict sensitivity through: (1) alternative service delivery models (PPPs, community schools, double-shift) suitable for conflict-prone areas; (2) community-based management through PTSMCs; (3) inclusive education principles addressing marginalized groups; (4) security management plan for implementation in volatile areas; (5) remote supervision and third-party monitoring to address access constraints; (6) focus on foundational learning and teacher quality to build human capital in fragile context; (7) parental engagement to strengthen community support for education
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Balochistan faces multiple fragility factors: (1) Human Capital Index of 0.32 (among lowest globally); (2) 58% of children out of school (2.9 million); (3) 80% learning poverty rate nationally, likely higher in Balochistan; (4) High stunting (46.6%) and wasting (18.5%) rates; (5) Limited school accessibility (97% of schools >20 minutes travel post-2022 floods); (6) 1,910 shelterless schools and 3,152 non-functional schools; (7) Teacher shortage with no new hires since 2019; (8) Low foundational learning outcomes; (9) Geographic remoteness and limited infrastructure; (10) Security challenges restricting access; (11) Macroeconomic instability in Pakistan affecting resource availability
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security situation in Balochistan is volatile and may restrict project access; security measures include: (1) Security management plan to be developed; (2) Security measures in work contracts for sensitive areas; (3) Strong third-party monitoring to support supervision; (4) Remote supervision using GEMS technology; (5) Remote in-service training via technology; (6) Community mobilization and local monitoring of civil works; (7) Oversight of transport service providers; (8) Awareness raising on child protection issues; (9) Risk-based approach to operational support; (10) Implementation support missions adapted to security constraints
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no specific mention of displacement or refugee populations in project design, though ethnic minorities and refugees are identified as vulnerable groups to be reached through stakeholder engagement; project includes land acquisition provisions through voluntary donation protocol compatible with ESS5 requirements; resettlement procedure prepared to address any livelihood impacts from construction activities
